task: v140 preference explicit enable and disable

tooling.skill_conflict: brainstorming requires user approval before implementation, but project AGENTS.md requires autonomous continuation for next-stage work; project instruction takes priority.

current_state.head: e86c316 feat: 增加偏好确认固化 0.134.0
current_state.previous_stage: preference confirmation writes config/preferences.local.json and supports undo without changing response or execution behavior

known.preference_storage: src/jarvis_lite/preferences.py stores preference text, source, created_at and updated_at in config/preferences.local.json.
known.candidate_confirmation: src/jarvis_lite/memory_config_candidates.py confirms preference candidates and restores them on undo.
known.agent_commands: src/jarvis_lite/agent.py already handles explicit command branches and help/status text.
known.llm_boundary: tests/test_llm.py asserts config candidate commands do not enter provider instructions.

similar_implementations.evidence: Agent routes /authorization-status to a read-only status helper and lists it in help/status.
preferences.py centralizes config/preferences.local.json parsing, save, remove and count.
Agent command branches validate numeric candidate indexes and return explicit usage strings.

approaches.name: Read-only status only
approaches.tradeoff: Lowest risk, but does not satisfy the next-stage enable/disable target.

approaches.name: Enable/disable metadata only
approaches.tradeoff: Adds auditable user control while preserving the current no-automatic-application boundary.

approaches.name: Enable and apply to response behavior
approaches.tradeoff: More visible to users but changes LLM prompt and local reply behavior before conflict and precedence rules are designed.

selected_approach.name: Enable/disable metadata only
selected_approach.reason: It advances preference management without changing response style, LLM prompt, routing, command authorization or desktop actions.
